scope.summary: CF3 migrates legacy audit state through verified capability directory handles, no-follow operations, identity checks, and an atomic create-new hard-link install that refuses destination replacement.
out_of_scope: The migration's layout policy and legacy/current conflict handling are unchanged.
No general filesystem sandbox or unrelated doctor repair behavior is claimed.

entry_points.name: apply_legacy_state_migration
entry_points.trace: The final migration fix f3650548 preserves the CF3 capability-handle design: apply_legacy_state_migration traverses parents with no-follow directory handles, writes 0600 create-new files, validates identities, and uses a hard link for the verified atomic install. legacy_migration_refuses_symlink_swaps_at_atomic_install deterministically replaces both the destination parent and final destination with symlinks; each case is refused without writing through the attacker-controlled replacement, while the legacy source remains unchanged.

entry_points.name: scoped core test-target compile
entry_points.trace: After proving doctor.rs unchanged from f3650548, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/var/tmp/oraclemcp-p0-close-evidence-target cargo check -p oraclemcp-core --tests --locked completed successfully.

limitations: This close correction makes no live-service claim; the deterministic filesystem race regression is Unix-only, matching the behavior it asserts.
The original evidence artifact was later deleted by an unrelated stale-index commit; this document restores the original bead's committed proof record and binding.
